title: Oreophylax serotinus
taxonomy: Oreophylax serotinus is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Ranunculaceae. It is a genus of orchids that is endemic to Mexico. This genus is part of the larger family Orchidaceae. Oreophylax serotinus is classified as an orchid and further classified within the Orchidaceae family as Orchidaceae subfamily Orchidoideae tribe Cranichideae subtribe Spiranthinae. The specific classification of this species is within the family Orchidaceae.
benefits: Oreophylax serotinus has been reported to have potential health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its effects on human health. However, some studies suggest that compounds found in Oreophylax serotinus may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. This plant may help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. Additionally, its compounds may have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in the prevention of infections. More studies are necessary to fully understand the health benefits of Oreophylax serotinus.
